content.problemStatement:
Toofan, a fearless tech prodigy, stumbles upon his most daring challenge yet ‚Äî hacking into **Advitiya's Security Vault**, the centerpiece of IIT Ropar‚Äôs annual tech fest. This vault is said to guard blueprints for groundbreaking exhibits and top-secret festival surprises.

Toofan knows that the vault's passcode contains NNN number to enter, with each number being an integer between 111 and KKK. While exploring, he found a scrap of paper that gave him some information about the passcode.

In particular, Toofan has with him an array AAA of length NNN, where each AiA_iAi‚Äã is either 000 or an integer between 111 and KKK.

Ai=0A_i = 0Ai‚Äã=0 means no information is known about the iii-th digit, and 1‚â§Ai‚â§K1 \leq A_i \leq K1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§K means the iii-th digit of the passcode is known to be AiA_iAi‚Äã.

The vault is a bit faulty: **it will open if either the correct code is entered, or the reverse of the correct code is entered.**

For example, if the vault's actual passcode is [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] then it will open even if [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] is entered.

In an attempt to open the vault, Toofan will try every possible passcode that matches the information he has, in lexicographic order. However, if he has already tried the reverse of a passcode before, he will skip the passcode since he knows about the vault being faulty (i.e. if he has tried [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] before, he will not try [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1]).

In the **worst case**, how many attempts must Toofan make before the vault is opened?

The answer can be large, so compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK: the length of the code, and the number of possible digits.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, denoting the information known to Toofan about the code.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer: the minimum number of passcodes Toofan must try, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Toofan has no information at all, and so needs to try every passcode. This will go as follows:

He will try [1,1],[1,2],[1,3][1, 1], [1, 2], [1, 3][1,1],[1,2],[1,3].
[2,1][2, 1][2,1] will be skipped since its reverse, [1,2][1, 2][1,2], was already tried.
He will then try [2,2],[2,3][2, 2], [2, 3][2,2],[2,3].
[3,1][3, 1][3,1] will be skipped since [1,3][1, 3][1,3] was already tried.
[3,2][3, 2][3,2] will be skipped since [2,3][2, 3][2,3] was already tried.
[3,3][3, 3][3,3] will be tried.

In total, 666 passcodes will be tried.
Test case 222: There are 7‚ãÖ7=497\cdot 7 = 497‚ãÖ7=49 possible passcodes that match the given pattern, and they all need to be tried.
